name: Runtime Governance for AI Agents
allowed_use_boundary: Use as external runtime path-governance crosswalk evidence.
Do not treat as certification, endorsement, execution authority, or commit-time authority.
claims: Runtime Governance for AI Agents can support execution-path and proposed-next-action comparison.
Policies on Paths can be mapped to runtime transition governance and fail-closed terms.
non_claims: Runtime path governance does not prove transition admissibility.
Runtime evaluation does not automatically grant execution authority.
The wiki relationship record is not acceptance of equivalence.

transition_table_mapping.claims: execution path evaluation, proposed next action, organizational state, runtime policy violation probability
transition_table_mapping.non_claims: no admissibility proof, certification, endorsement, or execution authority
transition_table_mapping.actor_or_authority_model: external runtime-governance posture; no StegVerse authority inherited
transition_table_mapping.evidence_model: execution path and organizational state evidence
transition_table_mapping.policy_or_rule_model: policies on paths and violation-probability review
transition_table_mapping.reconstruction_model: source plus crosswalk can reconstruct runtime-governance relationship limits
transition_table_mapping.SPE_overlap: may inform proposed-action review, not standing determination
transition_table_mapping.StegVerse_ecosystem_overlap: Runtime Transition Governance, Transition Table, Escalation, Fail-Closed
transition_table_mapping.fail_closed_conditions: missing source, undefined mapping, probabilistic overclaim, or authority overclaim

SPE_overlap.authority_evaluation: not performed by this wiki page

fail_closed_conditions: any certification, endorsement, execution authority, or commit-time authority claim appears
